name: l7_hole_emergency_gate_88
description: Switch to survival strategy when hole count exceeds 88 so wide-board runs stop prioritizing greedy clears in collapse-prone states.
llm_notes: Failure episodes average 99.66 holes vs 75.22 in successes (delta 24.44) while max height differs by only 0.41 (20.00 vs 19.59), so a hole-triggered mode gate should separate terminal trajectories earlier than height-based control.

name: l7_opening_hole_penalty_0_24_v4
description: Apply stronger hole suppression through pieces 0-24 to stabilize the fragile opening-to-midgame transition.
llm_notes: Failures end after 23.23 pieces on average with 99.66 holes, and outlier openings show lower early holes (78.8) than baseline early holes (85.7), indicating the high-risk window extends beyond the first 12 pieces.

name: l7_surface_volatility_brake_critical_height_85
description: Increase bumpiness penalty only when board height is in the danger band to focus anti-volatility pressure near terminal risk.
llm_notes: Bumpiness separates outcomes strongly (7.92 failure vs 1.44 success, delta 6.48), but height is near terminal in both groups, so gating bumpiness pressure to critical-height states should reduce over-constraint on safer boards.
